title: Some Stories about al-Mansur and His Conduct
description: According to $alih b. al-Wajih27-his father: Al-Mansur heard that 'Isa b. Musa had killed one of the descendants of Nasr b. Sayy,ir28O who was hiding in al-Kufah. `Isa was directed to him and cut off his head. Al-Mansur disapproved of that and was appalled. He was planning something that would be the destruction of `Isa, but `Isa's ignorance of the significance of what he had done stopped him. So he wrote to him: Were it not for the discernment and restraint of the Commander of the Faithful, your punishment for the killing of the son of Nasr b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 29
